slots.subject: ELA
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 2- Maps and Globes Lesson 12

objective.content_objective: We are learning facts about New Jersey.
objective.student_goal: I will tell facts about New Jersey.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform facts about New Jersey (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading) by using bilingual preview-review and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing a 3-5 fact summary that demonstrates use of key vocabulary and simple evidence.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Anticipatory Set: Activate Prior Knowledge - Using the Sentences and Fragments activity, students will share what they know about New Jersey.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Preview 6 key cognates in Portuguese-English (e.g., Nova Jersey - New Jersey; mapa - map; fatos - facts). 2) Quick pair share in Portuguese for 30 seconds, then one sentence aloud in English (graduate from L1 to L2).

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently. 
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis 
Ask students to periodically self-assess their own learning to give an idea of how much they accomplished over the course of the lesson

co_teaching_model.rationale: Typical Grade 3 class with Levels 3-4 dominant benefits from Station Teaching to provide structured language supports at one station while peers practice reading and evidence-finding at others.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: levels_3_4_dominant with some emerging Level 2 learners; mixed needs for reading comprehension and writing preparation

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce key vocabulary with Portuguese cognates and model one example aloud; lead a 90-second partner share in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Take attendance; display learning targets and quick directions; monitor transitions.
phase_plan.details: Use visual word cards with Portuguese-English cognates.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Pre-teach scavenger-hunt language, model how to find facts using the text, provide sentence frames in both languages.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read aloud chunked section and pose comprehension questions to whole group.
phase_plan.details: Stop after each chunk for think-pair-share; bilingual teacher supports pairs with targeted language.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a supported station: small-group guided reading using the Scavenger Hunt sheet with explicit bilingual prompts and cognate highlighting.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate a second station for independent text reading and evidence recording; circulate to check comprehension.
phase_plan.details: Third station: independent self-assessment and reading log completion.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead closure: call on volunteers to share one fact using a sentence frame; briefly highlight language progress.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick exit evidence and remind students of homework.
phase_plan.details: Use a visual timer for transitions and station rotations.

implementation_notes: Use existing Scavenger Hunt sheet, class text, and reading logs only; do not introduce new printed materials.
Prepare three station signs and a rotation chart; bilingual teacher prepares 6 cognate word cards and 3 sentence frames in English + Portuguese.
Use a visual timer and simple rotation chart to keep transitions smooth; bilingual teacher leads the supported station each rotation.

ell_support.implementation: Highlight high-frequency cognates (mapa/map, fatos/facts, Nova Jersey/New Jersey) on vocabulary cards; students match Portuguese cognates to English terms before reading and circle cognates in the text during station work.

Provide 3 simple sentence frames in Portuguese + English for the Scavenger Hunt (e.g., 'One fact is ___. / Um fato é ___.'; 'I read on page __ that __. / Eu li na página __ que __.'), post on chart for station use.
Use a two-column organizer (Fact / Page Number) with Portuguese labels to scaffold locating and recording facts during the Scavenger Hunt station.

special_needs_support: Provide a scribe or allow oral responses for students with writing difficulties during the Scavenger Hunt.
Offer highlighted text lines or shortened text chunks for students with processing needs.
materials: Class text about New Jersey (existing book)
Chart paper with sentence frames (English + Portuguese)

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may need support with the scavenger hunt sheet (i.e. provide page or fact number) due to the length of the text and the quantity of facts.
Students may be unfamiliar with the format of a friendly letter.
Students may struggle with sentence and paragraph structure.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often drops subject pronouns (e.g., 'É bonito' vs. 'It is beautiful'), which can lead students to write sentences without explicit subjects in English.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model and post explicit sentence frames showing subject + verb (e.g., 'It is ___.' / 'One fact is ___.'); practice oral frames before independent writing.

assessment.primary_assessment:
Daily Instructional Task:
Scavenger Hunt

Break students into small groups.

Have students take turns reading the text aloud.

Give each group a set of questions to answer.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Small-group Scavenger Hunt responses (written or orally recorded short answers in groups).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Inform; ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading + Speaking; Key Language Use: Inform (identify facts and cite page evidence). Target levels 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Use bilingual word bank, two-column organizer with Portuguese labels, allow oral answer in Portuguese then short English phrase using a frame.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide English sentence frames and highlighted text lines; encourage partner talk in English with brief Portuguese check-ins; require a short English phrase or sentence citing page number.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Require full English sentences citing evidence (page number) and one connective (because/por que) to link fact and source; minimal L1 support.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on content accuracy (correct fact cited), evidence use (page number or locator), and use of target vocabulary; for language score, expect phrase-level responses for Levels 1-2, sentence-level for 3-4, and connected sentences for 5-6.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses existing class text, Scavenger Hunt sheet, and classroom charts only.

homework.original_content:
Reading Log
SIPPS Group 2: Extension Lesson 13
homework.family_connection: Peça a um familiar para ler seu Reading Log com você. Em português, pergunte 2 fatos sobre Nova Jersey; depois, escreva as respostas em inglês. (Ask a family member to read your Reading Log with you. In Portuguese, ask 2 facts about New Jersey; then write the answers in English.)

slots.subject: ELA/SS
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit 3 Lesson 3
Sipps: Extension Review Lesson 9

objective.content_objective:
I can identify big story ideas based on what a character says and does.
I can support an opinion with text evidence.
objective.student_goal: I will find story ideas and cite evidence.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain character actions and support opinions with text evidence (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using preview–review and sentence frames with bilingual supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing short spoken or written responses that demonstrate comprehension and evidence use.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide
Unit 3
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Activate prior knowledge by naming cognates aloud: independente - independent; personagem - character; evidência - evidence. 2) Show the book cover and ask in Portuguese then English: 'O que a personagem pode sentir?' / 'What might the character feel?' Use cognates to connect ideas.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Stories read aloud to a small group. 

Small group instruction 
With ESL and Bilingual Teachers

Supporting resources provided

Sentence stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant inferred for 2nd grade; Station Teaching allows targeted scaffolded practice and L1 supports while maintaining whole-class access.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Typical 2nd-grade class with mixed proficiency but a majority in Levels 3-4 (developing). Several students at Levels 1-2 will rotate through high-support station.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick L1 preview of key vocabulary and cognates at a station; model sentence stems in Portuguese and English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the book and set the reading goal for the group in English.
phase_plan.details: Use visual of story map and 3-picture sequence to prompt predictions.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Co-read short paragraph with targeted students, stopping to translate key phrases and model think-aloud in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read aloud to the remaining small group, emphasize character actions and dialogue.
phase_plan.details: Both teachers highlight one sentence that shows a 'big idea'.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate a station where students use sentence frames (Portuguese/English) to state a character action and cite evidence; provide word/phrase cards.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Work with another station to guide students in identifying evidence and completing a mini story map.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate every 7 minutes; visual timer used.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support students who need one-on-one help writing a short sentence using a frame in English with Portuguese prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Observe and collect quick checks of student goal progress.
phase_plan.details: Students write or say one sentence showing a big idea and one piece of evidence.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model a closing sentence in Portuguese and translate to English; prompt a volunteer to share.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize learning and remind students of reading log homework.
phase_plan.details: Collect one exemplar for assessment.

implementation_notes: Use three stations: (1) Bilingual scaffold station (L1 previews, cognate cards); (2) Primary teacher guided reading and story map; (3) Independent practice with sentence stems and reading log.
Visual timers and simple rotation chart posted; bilingual teacher prepares sentence-stem cards in Portuguese+English before lesson.
Coordinate quick anecdotal note-taking: bilingual teacher notes language use; primary teacher notes comprehension evidence.
warnings: If planning time is limited (<45 min weekly), reduce station rotation by pairing two stations into one 15-minute block.
Room layout should allow three small areas; if space is tight, use parallel grouping instead.

ell_support.implementation: Provide simple bilingual frames on index cards: 'I think the big idea is ___ because ___ (evidence).'/ 'Eu acho que a ideia principal é ___ porque ___.' Use frames at the bilingual station and model them aloud.
Use a 3-box picture map (beginning/middle/end) with labels in Portuguese and English; students point and label with one phrase then form a sentence using a frame.
Introduce high-frequency cognates (independente/independent, personagem/character, evidência/evidence) and display matching cards; ask students to find cognates in text or think-aloud.
Briefly preview key vocabulary and a sentence in Portuguese before reading; after reading, review the sentence in English and have students repeat and point to evidence in the text.

special_needs_support: Provide one-line sentence starter and allow oral response recorded by teacher.
Seat students near the bilingual teacher for simplified directions and repetition.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may think they can only read at their desk or in the library. 
Students may not understand what the word independent means. 
Students may not be able to work independently.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often omits subject pronouns when the verb form indicates the subject; students may produce English sentences like 'Is reading' instead of 'He/She is reading.'
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model short complete sentences with subject pronouns in English and contrast with Portuguese usage; use choral repetition and sentence frames that explicitly include the subject (He/She).

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be assessed based on the reading goal they set for themselves. This goal will allow the teacher to determine the students' understanding of what a goal means and their ability to write in a complete sentence.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Reading-goal self-assessment written or oral response (teacher collects one exemplar during closure).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Key Language Use: Explain + Inform; ELD domains: Speaking/Writing/Reading; Target levels: Levels 1-4 (2-3 cluster).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow oral response in Portuguese then produce a single English phrase using a provided frame; teacher scribe the English sentence.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide bilingual sentence frames and word banks; students write one sentence with text evidence and underline the evidence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students write a complete sentence or two in English citing two pieces of text evidence; encourage use of connectors like 'because'.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on presence of a clear idea statement and at least one piece of evidence; at lower levels credit phrase-level evidence and correct identification of evidence even if grammar is imperfect.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses existing reading logs and sentence stems; task unchanged.

homework.original_content:
Read for 20 minutes and log

Spelling (ABC Order)
homework.family_connection: Peça ao seu filho para ler por 20 minutos e, em português, conte quem é a personagem e uma coisa que ela faz. Then have child tell one sentence in English using the sentence frame and record it in the reading log.

slots.subject: Science
slots.unit_lesson:
Properties of Matter
Lesson 4 Day 4

objective.content_objective: I am learning about the properties of different materials
objective.student_goal: I will describe materials using words and examples
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform properties of materials (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading) by using bilingual vocabulary previews and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing labeled examples and simple descriptions that demonstrate understanding of observable properties.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to 2nd grade science curriculum starting with page 126 
 2nd Grade Science
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Show three pictured materials with Portuguese cognates (plastic - plástico; metal - metal; wood - madeira) and say each name in Portuguese then English. 2) Ask students in pairs (one Portuguese, one English) to point and say the word aloud in both languages to build vocabulary recognition.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant inferred for typical 2nd grade; Station Teaching allows differentiated language scaffolds and targeted L1 support during hands-on material sorting.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class inferred as Levels 3-4 dominant with some Levels 1-2 newcomers; mixed but majority developing ELLs in grade 2 cluster.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead brief bilingual vocabulary preview using visuals and cognates in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the day's driving question and demonstrate one example.
phase_plan.details: Use 3 picture cards with cognates visible; students repeat choral responses.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model sentence frames in Portuguese→English and check comprehension with gesture and visuals.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate material properties with manipulatives while naming properties in English.
phase_plan.details: Focus on observable properties: color, texture, hardness, bend.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Circulate to the L1-support station: pre-teach target words, assist Level 1-2 students to complete labels in Portuguese then translate key words to English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Manage two other stations with guided practice and challenge tasks for Levels 3-4 and 5-6.
phase_plan.details: Three stations: (A) L1-supported labeling and sort (bilingual teacher), (B) Guided description with sentence frames (primary), (C) Extension: compare two materials and record findings (primary). Rotate every 8 minutes.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Elicit one bilingual example from students and display a quick exit visual (thumbs up + one word in English).
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick-check responses and summarize main idea.
phase_plan.details: Use a shared anchor chart with visuals and 3 target words (in both languages).

implementation_notes: Prepare three station signs (A/B/C) and simple rotation chart; use visual timer for 8-minute rotations.
Bilingual teacher prepares small set of picture-word cards with Portuguese cognates and a mini word bank for Levels 1-2.
Primary teacher prepares manipulatives referenced in the original lesson and the Be a Scientist Notebook pages.
warnings: If planning time is limited (<45 min), simplify station materials to pre-made picture-word cards to reduce prep.
If classroom space is tight, reduce to two stations and use pair rotations instead of three simultaneous stations.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce 6 high-utility cognates (plástico / plastic; metal / metal; madeira / wood; duro / hard; macio / soft; transparente / transparent) on a two-column chart. Students point and repeat in Portuguese then English during warmup.
Provide simple frames on index cards: 'This material is ___.' and 'It feels ___.' Practice orally with choral and pair repetition; encourage Portuguese→English bridging for Level 1-2.
Use picture cards and manipulatives labeled in Portuguese and English; students match labels during station work, supporting word-picture mapping.
Students think in Portuguese if needed, share with partner in Portuguese, then share a short English sentence aloud using a frame; teacher models translation process.

special_needs_support: Provide one-on-one picture cue cards and allow scribing for students with fine-motor challenges.
Offer shortened task: choose and label two materials instead of four for students requiring reduced load.
materials: Manipulatives (small samples of plastic, metal, wood, fabric)

misconceptions.original_content: Students may confuse what properties are.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may translate expressions like 'depends on' differently and omit the preposition or use verb forms that shift meaning (e.g., 'depende de' → 'depend on' errors).
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach the phrase 'depends on' with a visual cause-effect chart and practice frame: 'Whether it floats depends on the material.' Give repeated bilingual examples.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will complete p. 46 in their Be a Scientist Notebook

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Notebook page p.46 (written labels and short descriptions) - same task, teacher observes and collects
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Inform + ELD-LA.2-3 (Reading/Writing) - target Levels 1-4; product: labeled items + 1-2 short descriptions

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to label pictures using provided bilingual word bank and draw to show property; teacher or peer can scribe short English label; permit Portuguese label with arrow to English cognate.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a mini word bank; students write one sentence in English using frame (e.g., 'This material is hard.'), with bilingual teacher conferencing as needed.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students write two descriptive sentences comparing materials using connectors (e.g., 'and', 'but'); minimal scaffolding.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on observable language features: correct targeted vocabulary usage (may be in English or Portuguese for Levels 1-2), ability to attach a correct property to a material, and use of at least one English sentence or phrase for Levels 3-4.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses only Be a Scientist Notebook p.46 and classroom visuals/manipulatives referenced in lesson.

homework.family_connection: Peça a uma pessoa da família para mostrar dois objetos em casa e dizer em português que material é (por exemplo: 'plástico' e 'madeira'). Em inglês, child practices saying the material word and one property (e.g., 'plastic - light').

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: Lesson 5: Measure in Meters

objective.content_objective: I can learn a meter is a larger unit of measurement.
objective.student_goal: I will compare lengths using meters.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain measurement and compare lengths (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using bilingual sentence frames, manipulatives, and cognate scaffolds appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing short spoken or written comparisons that demonstrate correct unit vocabulary and comparative language.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 3: Measuring Length
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Step 1: Show a meter stick labeled 'metro' and say the Portuguese word aloud (metro). Step 2: Use a familiar classroom object and ask in Portuguese 'Quanto mede?' then model the English sentence 'It is ___ meters' so students hear both forms. Portuguese cognates/terms: metro, medir.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Small group instruction,
Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Default to levels_3_4_dominant: Station Teaching allows simultaneous differentiated practice and targeted L1/L2 scaffolds for small groups while preserving whole-class input.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Typical 2nd-grade class with a dominant band of Levels 3-4 and several students at Levels 1-2 who benefit from hands-on bilingual stations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a quick bilingual vocabulary review (metro, medir, mais longo/mais curto) with gestures.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Quick whole-class prompt and model one example measurement.
phase_plan.details: Use a shared visual and point to unit labels.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model sentence frames in Portuguese and English; pre-teach key prepositions and comparison phrases.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate measuring with a meter stick and counting cubes for a sample object.
phase_plan.details: Show explicit mapping between Portuguese terms and English frames.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate Station A (bilingual scaffold): preview/review in Portuguese, guided practice with sentence frames and manipulatives.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate Station B (math focus): problem-solving practice with numberlines and independent measurement tasks.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate through Station C (visuals) with a peer checklist; use visual timer for 6-7 minute rotations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Elicit 1-2 quick bilingual check-outs using target frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick whole-class evidence and restate the learning target.
phase_plan.details: Use a one-sentence oral exit from 2 pairs of students.

implementation_notes: Prepare 3 station cards (Bilingual station, Math practice, Visual/Manipulative station) using only classroom materials listed.
Use a visual timer and rotation chart so transitions take <1 minute.
Bilingual teacher pre-teaches and circulates in Station A to ensure L1 scaffolding then supports language at other stations.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce and display Portuguese terms (metro, centímetro, medir) next to English labels; explicitly point out similarities and differences. Use quick partner checks in Portuguese then produce English phrase.
Provide frames on index cards: 'This object is ___ meters long.' and 'It is longer than ___.' Model in Portuguese, then English; students practice with manipulatives.
Pair a consistent hand gesture for 'longer/shorter' and show numberline visuals; encourage students to use gestures as they say the sentence frame aloud in English.
Before each station rotation, quickly preview steps in Portuguese (1–2 sentences) and then review the English frame after practice; this supports transfer and comprehension.

special_needs_support: Provide a high-contrast, enlarged ruler or numberline and reduce the number of items to measure.
Seat students near the teacher for guided one-on-one modeling and keep manipulatives within easy reach.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle with understanding why groups are getting different answers.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese prepositions and constructions for measurements can differ (e.g., 'medir de ___ até ___' vs. English 'measure from ___ to ___'), which may lead to confusion about start/end points.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach a consistent bilingual frame for start and end points (e.g., 'measure from ___ to ___ / medir de ___ até ___') and practice with manipulatives marking both ends.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.3.1 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool Down quick written/oral exit: measure one classroom item and state its length using cubes or a meter stick (can be oral or with manipulatives).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain (compare lengths) + ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking/Listening; target WIDA Levels 1-4 (word/phrase → sentence).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide bilingual word bank (metro, medir), allow pointing to manipulatives, permit one-word or phrase responses in English or Portuguese, and allow teacher scribing.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame cards and a modeled example; encourage students to use a short English sentence with the unit.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Ask students to write or say a two-sentence comparison using connectors (because, so) and accurate units.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score math accuracy separately from language: math = correct measurement within allowed tolerance; language = use of unit vocabulary and at least a phrase or sentence that conveys comparison.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; use counting cubes, numberlines, meter stick, and visuals already referenced.

homework.original_content: Compare two objects at home and note which is longer.
homework.family_connection: Peça a alguém em casa para escolher dois objetos e pergunte 'Qual é mais longo?'. Escreva ou desenhe a resposta e diga a palavra 'metro' ou 'centímetro'. / Ask someone at home to pick two objects and ask 'Which is longer?'. Draw or write the answer and say 'meter' or 'centimeter'.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: LESSON 1: REPRESENT NUMBERS IN DIFFERENT WAYS3.3.1 Teacher Guide

objective.content_objective: SWBAT represent numbers using base-ten blocks, base-ten diagrams, expanded form, numerals, and word form.
objective.student_goal: I will show numbers in different forms.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform representations of three-digit numbers and explain equivalence (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Writing) by using translanguaging, sentence frames, and visual organizers appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing written labels and a short verbal explanation that demonstrates understanding of place value and representation.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Which Three Go Together: Numbers within 1,000 (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Quick preview of key vocabulary in Portuguese (base-ten → base dez; expanded form → forma expandida; diagram → diagrama). 2) Show a base-ten block model and say the vocabulary first in Portuguese, then in English; invite students to repeat both terms.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Activity 1: Introduce Connecting Cubes, Explore (15 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to revisit numbers that are written in different forms. Students match numbers represented in different forms: base-ten numerals, base-ten diagrams, number names, and expanded form.

Activity 2: Numbers in Different Forms Round Table (20 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to use place-value understanding from grade 2 to decompose numbers in different ways. In small groups, students start by writing a three-digit number, and then pass their number to the group member at their right. Each time students receive the number, they decompose it in a different way.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant (typical 3rd-grade distribution); stations allow targeted scaffolds for Entering/Emerging students while extending practice for Developing students.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Typical 3rd-grade class with a mix of WIDA Levels 1–4, majority Levels 3–4, some newcomers (Level 1) who need concrete L1 previews.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead quick Portuguese-English vocabulary preview and model one matching example aloud in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Circulate, monitor prior knowledge and check understanding of instructions.
phase_plan.details: Use visual cards showing numeral, diagram, expanded form, and word form.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate one station task in Portuguese and provide sentence frames in English for matching (I see __ = Eu vejo __).
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model decomposition strategy with base-ten blocks and think-aloud in English.
phase_plan.details: Model mapping between blocks, diagram, expanded form, and number name.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Rotate between stations focusing on Students Levels 1-3: provide L1 preview, point to cognates, scaffold sentence frames, and monitor accuracy.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Support Stations for Levels 3-4 and extend challenge tasks; run an analysis station for students ready to create expanded form from diagrams.
phase_plan.details: Three stations: (A) Matching cards (B) Build with cubes and draw diagram (C) Write expanded form and word name.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a quick bilingual share (1 student in Portuguese, 1 in English) using a shared sentence frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick exit evidence and note students needing re-teach.
phase_plan.details: Use exit ticket: match 1 number to 3 representations.

implementation_notes: Use visual station signs with Portuguese labels for newcomers; keep rotations short and use a timer visible to all students.
Prepare matching card sets (numeral, diagram, expanded form, word name) in advance; use the same cards across stations to avoid new materials.
Coordinate signals for transitions (music, visual timer) and assign roles in each group (reader, builder, recorder).

ell_support.implementation: Point out Portuguese-English cognates (e.g., 'diagram' → 'diagrama', 'decimal'/'dezena' connections) during station introductions; highlight cognates on cards with a small dot.

Provide frames for oral and written use: 'The number ___ has __ hundreds, __ tens, and __ ones.' and Portuguese parallel: 'O número ___ tem __ centenas, __ dezenas e __ unidades.' Students copy and use frames in practice.
Use a three-column organizer (Hundreds | Tens | Ones) with picture, numeral, and word form cues; bilingual labels added to support comprehension.
Before practice rotations, provide a 1–2 minute Portuguese mini-explanation for newcomers and allow short L1 use for planning answers, then require English recording using frames.

special_needs_support: Seat student near teacher during stations and provide a number line strip as a physical prompt.
Allow the use of manipulatives throughout and provide extra time for station rotations.
materials: Matching cards (numeral, diagram, expanded form, word form)

misconceptions.original_content: Misconception: Students may not understand place value and need a mini lesson on ones, tens, and hundreds.

linguistic_note.note: In Portuguese descriptive words often follow the noun (e.g., 'número grande'), but in English adjectives precede nouns ('large number'); students may produce awkward English phrases when describing representations.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model and provide short adjective-noun frames in English and Portuguese side-by-side; have students repeat the short English frame aloud before writing.

assessment.primary_assessment: 3.3.1 Cool-down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down exit ticket: match a three-digit numeral to its base-ten diagram, expanded form, and written number name (paper/pencil).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a partially completed organizer (fill-in-the-blank), allow student to point to correct card and say the number in Portuguese before writing a single-word numeral or phrase in English.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame for written response and allow students to draw base-ten blocks then write the expanded form using the frame.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect full written label in English with explanation using connectors (because, so) and ask for one example of an alternative decomposition.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score primarily for correct mapping between representations; award language points for use of provided sentence frames and accuracy of number words (0–2 language points).

slots.subject: ELA

objective.content_objective:
We are learning facts about New Jersey.
We are learning to write about important facts.
objective.student_goal: I will write five facts in a letter.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform and Narrate facts about New Jersey (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Writing) by using sentence frames, cognate support, and guided drafting appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing a 5-fact friendly letter with simple organization and cited facts.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Anticipatory Set: Anticipatory Set: Review components of a friendly letter and facts learned yesterday. Use your scavenger hunt to help remember facts.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Show Portuguese cognates related to letter features (cabeçalho/header, saudação/greeting, despedida/closing). 2) Quick bilingual model: teacher composes the greeting aloud in Portuguese then translates to English.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently. 
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis 
Ask students to periodically self-assess their own learning to give an idea of how much they accomplished over the course of the lesson

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching allows explicit language support for writing at one station (bilingual drafting) while other stations focus on fact retrieval and organization, aligning with WIDA scaffolding for Grades 2-3.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: levels_3_4_dominant with some Level 2 emerging writers needing sentence-level scaffolds

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick review of friendly letter components with Portuguese cognates and model a sample opening sentence using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Display writing checklist and target rubric; explain the task expectations.
phase_plan.details: Show one exemplar friendly letter on chart paper (bilingual labels).

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model drafting one paragraph using SPO (Subject, Point, One Evidence) frames in both languages; coach students to plan 5 facts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Ask comprehension questions and provide evidence prompts from yesterday's readings.
phase_plan.details: Use a shared write for first two sentences; bilingual teacher highlights connectors.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead the supported writing station: guided drafting with sentence frames, cognate hints, and peer-check in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Monitor independent writers and the evidence-citing station, conference with groups about facts.
phase_plan.details: Rotate groups through the three stations: drafting, fact-check, independent writing.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Collect one volunteer writer to read a paragraph aloud; give specific language praise and correction in brief Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Remind students of criteria and homework; collect drafts if required.
phase_plan.details: Use exit card: one fact plus page number.

implementation_notes: Ensure the drafting station has printed sentence frames in both languages and a bilingual exemplar.
Rotate students in 6-8 minute shifts; bilingual teacher supports the same small group for fidelity.
Use evidence-based spo (SPO) frames posted at stations; do not create new printed packets.

ell_support.implementation: Provide 5 frames mapped to friendly letter parts (greeting, opening fact, body facts, closing) in English with Portuguese translations; students copy frames into drafts then replace blanks with facts.
Pair stronger bilingual peers with emerging writers; the stronger peer helps translate the student's Portuguese idea into an English sentence using frames.
Provide a short cognate list (e.g., facts - fatos, map - mapa, river - rio) to help students find English words for their facts during drafting.

special_needs_support: Allow use of a scribe or voice-to-text for students with fine-motor or written-language challenges.
Provide a checklist with visuals for friendly letter parts to support organization.
materials: Scavenger Hunt notes from previous lesson
Friendly letter exemplar on chart paper
Sentence frame strips in English + Portuguese

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may need support with the scavenger hunt sheet (i.e. provide page or fact number) due to the length of the text and the quantity of facts.
Students may be unfamiliar with the format of a friendly letter.
Students may struggle with sentence and paragraph structure.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese places adjectives after nouns more often than English (e.g., 'cidade grande' vs. 'big city'), which can lead to word order errors in English descriptions.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Explicitly model English noun + adjective order in sample sentences and have students reorder matched Portuguese phrases into English word order during guided practice.

assessment.primary_assessment:
Daily Instructional Task:
5 Fact Friendly Letter

Pretend a friend is coming to visit NJ and you want to tell him/her about it. 
Write a friendly letter and include at least 5 facts from the book about New Jersey.  
Using the SPO activity, describe New Jersey’s unique land features. 
Write facts and cite evidence from the text to support your facts.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Independent 5-Fact Friendly Letter (written product) assessed with primary rubric for content and organization.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Inform + Narrate; ELD-LA.2-3.Inform/Writing + Speaking; Key Language Uses: Inform and Narrate; target levels 2-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide sentence starters and frames in Portuguese+English, allow picture labeling of facts, permit oral rehearsal in Portuguese before writing one short English sentence per fact.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide English sentence frames, cognate list, and a checklist to organize five facts into a letter; expect sentence-level responses with a closing.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect connected sentences for each fact with a brief evidence citation; encourage use of transition words (because, também) with minimal L1 support.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Content: inclusion of 5 accurate facts with evidence; Organization: recognizable friendly letter format; Language: sentence-level clarity for Levels 3-4, phrase-level for 1-2; vocabulary use of target terms.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses existing Scavenger Hunt notes, classroom exemplar, and sentence-frame strips.

objective.content_objective:
I can support an explanation using the definition of a key term and text evidence.
I can make predictions before reading a text.
objective.student_goal: I will define key terms and predict text events.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain a key term and make predictions about a text (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using preview–review, visuals, and bilingual frames appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a short prediction and a definition with text-based justification.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide
Unit 3
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Introduce the vocabulary 'growth mindset' and related cognates: crescimento - growth; mentalidade - mindset. 2) Show a short picture and ask in Portuguese then English: 'O que pode acontecer a seguir?' / 'What will happen next?'.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Students will be provided with visuals to help assist them with understanding what a growth mindset is. 

Small group instruction. 
With ESL and Bilingual Teachers

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports vocabulary preview and visual scaffolds for students across proficiency levels while allowing the bilingual teacher to lead a high-support vocabulary station.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed proficiency with several developing readers (Levels 3-4) and some emerging students (Levels 1-2); visuals help bridge meaning.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce the key term 'growth mindset' in Portuguese and English and show a simple visual example.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Pose a prediction prompt about the story cover and model making a prediction.
phase_plan.details: Use a picture card and a short think-pair-share in mixed language pairs.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Teach vocabulary station: matching visuals to definitions with Portuguese cognates and gesture.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read the opening pages aloud, pausing to ask prediction questions.
phase_plan.details: Students mark predicted events on a sticky note.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support a small group making predictions and writing a one-sentence definition using a frame in English with L1 prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Work with another group to find text evidence supporting predictions.
phase_plan.details: Rotate groups every 7 minutes; use sentence-stem cards.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Conference briefly with students needing language support to record their prediction in the reading log.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Circulate and check accuracy of predictions and definitions.
phase_plan.details: Students draw a prediction picture and write one sentence.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Invite one student to share prediction in Portuguese then in English using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize how definition and evidence helped prediction.
phase_plan.details: Link to homework reading log and spelling practice.

implementation_notes: Prepare visual definition cards for 'growth mindset' and synonyms in Portuguese/English.
Use colored sticky notes for predictions; bilingual teacher labels visuals in both languages.
Ensure sentence-stem cards highlight 'I predict...' and 'Growth mindset means...'.

ell_support.implementation: Create 4–6 picture-definition cards with the Portuguese term, English term, and a short example; students match pictures to definitions at the bilingual station.
Students first discuss prediction in Portuguese with a partner, then share in English using a scaffolded frame: 'I predict ___ because ___.'.
Provide a simple frame: 'Growth mindset means ___. An example is ___.' in Portuguese and English; practice orally then record.
Use consistent gestures when introducing vocabulary and prediction language to build multimodal memory; encourage students to use gestures while speaking.

special_needs_support: Provide picture choices for predictions and allow circling instead of writing.
Allow extended time for writing and permit oral recording if needed.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may not understand how to use decoding strategies without prompting. In
Students may not be able to read independently.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often places adjectives after nouns (e.g., 'casa grande'), which can confuse English adjective order when students describe characters or settings.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model adjective–noun order in English with comparison sentences (English vs. Portuguese), and practice with picture prompts (e.g., 'big house' vs 'casa grande').

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be monitored while they are shopping for books, the teacher will assess them based off their ability to independently do this task. In addition to their independence while shopping for books, the teacher will monitor their ability to read independently.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Teacher observation checklist during book-shopping and independent reading time (running record of independence behaviors).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Key Language Use: Inform + Explain; ELD domains: Speaking/Reading; Target Levels: 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Use picture-choice prompts and allow student to point to book features; teacher models how to say a book title in English and Portuguese.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence starters for requesting a book: 'I want a book about __ because __.' and prompt students to state reading preference and a reason.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students independently explain choice and make a short prediction about the book using evidence from the cover.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for demonstrated independence behaviors (selecting a book, stating a reason/prediction) and presence of a prediction or justification; language accuracy secondary to independence.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials; uses existing classroom book corner and observation routines.

homework.original_content:
Read for 20 minutes and log

Spelling (color code-constants and vowels)
homework.family_connection: Peça para a família perguntar em português: 'O que você acha que vai acontecer?' Then ask the child to say the prediction in English and write one sentence in the reading log. Use colored points to show consonants and vowels.

objective.content_objective: I am learning about the properties of different materials
objective.student_goal: I will sort materials and say one property
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform how materials differ (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Writing) by using bilingual sorting, sentence frames, and visual organizers appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing labeled sorts and short written/photo-supported notes that show understanding of properties.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to 2nd grade science curriculum starting with page 126
 2nd Grade Science
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Review yesterday's cognates (plástico, metal, madeira) with flash visuals. 2) Introduce two new cognates (vidro / glass; papel / paper) and have students point to real examples or pictures while saying both words.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching continues to provide targeted bilingual support for students still acquiring core vocabulary while other students extend learning through guided comparisons.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with a subset of Levels 1-2; stations allow the bilingual teacher to pre-teach and reinforce vocabulary in Portuguese and scaffold language production.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick review of previous vocabulary using a two-column cognate chart and gesture cues.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Review learning target and model sorting action.
phase_plan.details: Students echo words twice in choral response.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model 'how to sort' using Portuguese then English prompts and demonstrate one sort with manipulatives.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce sorting criteria cards and model thinking aloud.
phase_plan.details: Discuss why a material goes in one pile using simple frames.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run L1-support station: guide small group to sort and label items, prompting Portuguese→English transfer and supporting writing/ drawing.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Manage two stations for guided practice and challenge tasks; circulate and prompt higher-order comparisons.
phase_plan.details: Rotation every 8 minutes; use gestures and visuals for newcomers.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask one student in Portuguese to tell the class which material they chose and what property it has, then model English sentence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick exit checks and note students needing support.
phase_plan.details: Post one sample from each station on the board.

implementation_notes: Bring real-life classroom items for sorting (paper, plastic, metal piece, small glass sample if safe).
Bilingual teacher prepares mini word bank and pictorial sorting cards.
warnings: Ensure safety for any glass samples; substitute picture if necessary.

ell_support.implementation: Before station rotations, preview vocabulary in Portuguese and English; after rotations, review key words and have students show where they placed items and say a word in English.
Provide a two-column sorting mat labeled in Portuguese and English; students sort items and draw or write one word in English for their column.
Teacher models describing a material using a frame in English, then repeats in Portuguese, showing translation and structure.
Pair students so one stronger English speaker supports vocabulary while bilingual teacher supports L1 explanations for clarity.

special_needs_support: Provide enlarged picture cards and allow extra time for sorting tasks.
Offer checklist steps for students who need task sequencing support.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may be confused on how to differentiate materials.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often places adjectives after nouns (e.g., 'madeira dura' vs. English 'hard wood'), which can cause word order errors when students form English phrases.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model and practice correct English word order with sentence frames and contrast examples: 'hard wood' vs. 'made wood (incorrect)'. Use choral repetition.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will complete p.47 in their Be a Scientist Notebook.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Notebook page p.47 (sorting and short responses) - same format collected by teacher
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Inform + ELD-LA.2-3 (Writing/Speaking) - target Levels 1-4; product: sorted items annotated and one short phrase/label.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow picture matching and labeling in Portuguese with arrow to English cognate; teacher can scribe English phrase during conference.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame and word bank; students write one English label and draw evidence of property.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students write a comparative sentence explaining differences between two materials using connector 'because'.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Check correct property-to-material matching, evidence of sorting logic, and use of at least one English label or phrase for expected levels.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials; uses Be a Scientist Notebook p.47 and classroom manipulatives only.

homework.family_connection: Peça ao seu filho para encontrar um objeto em casa e dizer em português de que material é (por exemplo: 'papel'). Em inglês, pratique juntos dizendo: 'This is paper. It is light.'

objective.content_objective: I can solve story problems using length.
objective.student_goal: I will solve length story problems.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain and solve length story problems (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing) by using bilingual problem frames, manipulatives, and visual organizers appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a labeled sketch or sentence that shows measurement reasoning.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 3: Measuring Length
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Step 1: Read a short two-sentence story in Portuguese that involves measuring (e.g., 'O gato mede 2 metros?'). Step 2: Show the English version and highlight cognates/terms (comprimento → length). Portuguese cognates/terms: comprimento, medir.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Small group instruction,
Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports differentiated scaffolds for story-problem language and calculation practice while preserving time for bilingual previews and guided problem-solving.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with a subset of Levels 1-2 needing bilingual previews for story vocabulary.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce story vocabulary and act out the story in Portuguese then map to English frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read the English story problem and model one solve-aloud.
phase_plan.details: Use a simple two-sentence story and gestures to show actions.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model how to identify 'what to measure' and select units in Portuguese and English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate drawing a labeled sketch and using manipulatives.
phase_plan.details: Highlight signal words in story problems (how long, how far).

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead Station A with L1 scaffolds: sentence frames and guided practice on 2-step story problems.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead Station B practicing measurement calculations and check-ins; circulate to assess understanding.
phase_plan.details: Station C: independent drawing/recording with teacher checklist.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Elicit one bilingual student explanation of their solution using both L1 and English frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize problem-solving steps and key vocabulary.
phase_plan.details: Select 1-2 students to share with class.

implementation_notes: Provide story cards with simple pictures; keep text short for 2nd grade reading level.
Coordinate which station holds which materials so transitions are smooth.
Use paired Portuguese-English retell for students at Levels 1-2 before producing English response.

ell_support.implementation: Provide a short illustrated story card and ask students to draw the objects and mark lengths; display bilingual labels (comprimento/length).
Highlight shared roots and related vocabulary (medir/measure, comprimento/length) and use them to build a quick word bank.
Model aloud how to count along a numberline to represent the story; provide bilingual prompts and encourage students to verbalize steps.
Pair a stronger English speaker with a Portuguese speaker to retell the problem in Portuguese then produce the English answer together using frames.

special_needs_support: Pre-cut manipulatives or pre-drawn numberline for students who need fine-motor support.
Provide one-step chunking of the story problem and allow extra think time.

misconceptions.original_content:
Misconceptions: Students may need materials cut for them. 

Students may not know how to properly use math materials to assist their learning.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often places adjectives after nouns (e.g., 'mesa longa') which can cause word-order confusion when students form English phrases like 'long table'.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Practice target English frame order with visuals: highlight the English pattern 'long table' and provide bilingual sorting activities to contrast orders.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be assessed on their responses for the cool down activity.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Teacher-collected cool down responses: short answer or manipulatives showing solution to a story problem (oral or written).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain/Inform + ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing & Speaking; target Levels 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to demonstrate with manipulatives and point to a labeled picture; accept a picture or one-word label in English or Portuguese.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a drawing template; expect a short sentence describing the measurement.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a brief written explanation including the measurement and reasoning using connector words.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on problem representation (picture/manipulative), correct unit selection, and use of at least one measurement term (length/comprimento/meters).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; use story cards, counting cubes, numberlines, and visuals already referenced.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: LESSON 2: ADDITION AND SUBTRACTION SITUATIONS  3.3.2 Teacher Guide

objective.content_objective: SWBAT use addition and subtraction to solve problems within 1,000.
objective.student_goal: I will solve addition and subtraction problems within 1,000.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain strategies for solving addition and subtraction problems within 1,000 (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing) by using math-talk frames, visual models, and bilingual previews appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a written equation and a short explanation.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Notice and Wonder: Two Curious Tables (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Introduce key verbs in Portuguese and English: addition → adição, subtraction → subtração, total → total; 2) Use a quick two-group demonstration in Portuguese then English to show 'notice' and 'wonder' about a problem picture.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Activity 1: Falls and Monuments (25 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to solve word problems that involve adding or subtracting numbers within 1,000, using approaches with which they are familiar from earlier grades.

Activity 2: Journal About Connections (10 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to reflect on the strategies they used in the first activity. This is an opportunity to check in with students about the strategies from grade 2 that they are comfortable using and those they find more challenging.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Parallel Teaching allows smaller teacher-led groups to reduce language load for newcomers while maintaining grade-level problem complexity for majority students (Levels 3–4 dominant).
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed proficiency with some Level 1s; splitting class into two parallel groups improves teacher-student ratio for scaffolding.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a Portuguese strategic preview of the two word-problem pictures and pre-teach 2–3 verbs and keywords.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead the English 'Notice and Wonder' whole-class discussion and model problem-reading strategies.
phase_plan.details: Use picture cues and keyword cards.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model solving one simpler problem in Portuguese with a gradual translation to English and show a number sentence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model a more complex solution strategy in English (e.g., making tens, decomposing).
phase_plan.details: Show both numeric and pictorial representations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with a small group of students needing language and conceptual support (Levels 1-3): provide guided translation, sentence stems, and manipulatives.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Work with the other group pushing reasoning and multiple strategies (Levels 3-4), circulating to extend thinking.
phase_plan.details: Rotate groups mid-practice (about 12 min each) so all students get bilingual scaffold.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support students who need help drafting one sentence in English for their journal using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect journals and facilitate 1-2 share-outs in English.
phase_plan.details: Students complete a short journal entry: 'I solved by ___ because ___.'.

implementation_notes: Use number lines and base-ten blocks at tables; bilingual teacher prepares a small card with key words in Portuguese/English for targeted group.
Group lists and rotation schedule prepared in advance; plan which students will receive bilingual small-group support.
Keep problem sets identical across groups to preserve content fidelity.

ell_support.implementation: Provide stems such as 'I added ___ and ___ because ___' and Portuguese equivalents; display stems at group table and require one oral rehearsal in Portuguese then English.
Encourage drawing pictures or number lines to represent word problems; bilingual teacher models drawing and labels in Portuguese and English.
Highlight key action words (total, change, difference) on strips with Portuguese translation; students glue the correct word strip under the problem before solving.
Provide two scaffolded stems for journal writing and allow students to copy the English stem after oral rehearsal in Portuguese.

special_needs_support: Provide a one-page graphic organizer with step boxes (read, draw, solve, write) and allow extra time for completion.
Allow use of manipulatives and reduce number of problems for students with processing needs.

misconceptions.original_content: Misconception: Students may not line up place value correctly when applying addition algorithms.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese uses different prepositional constructions (e.g., 'depende de') which can lead to literal translation errors in English explanations (e.g., 'depend on' confusion) and awkward phrasing in describing steps.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model the English explanation structure for problem-solving steps and provide short bilingual connectors on the board (because → porque; so → então).

assessment.primary_assessment:
3.3.2 Cool-down
Item UIN - VH094716

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down exit ticket: one addition/subtraction word problem within 1,000 (paper) requiring equation and short explanation.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain; ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading+Writing; targeted levels 1-4

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide the equation template with blanks (___ + ___ = ___) and allow student to point to drawn model or say the answer in Portuguese; permit one-word English responses or number-only answers.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stem 'I solved by ___ because ___' and allow students to use drawings and number sentences; expect a short English phrase using the stem.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a full written explanation in English with evidence and possibly an alternate strategy comparison.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Grade for correct computation first; then award language credit for use of sentence stems, correct math vocabulary, and clarity of explanation (0–2 language points).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No extra materials or task changes introduced; supports are scaffolds and sentence stems only.
