homework.family_connection: Peça para seu filho(a) explicar em português como resolveu um problema de soma ou subtração e, em seguida, escrevam juntos a equação em inglês. Use objetos da casa (ex.: tampas) para representar a história.

slots.subject: ELA
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 2- Maps and Globes Lesson 14 Day 1

objective.content_objective:
I am learning how to use map features to understand information.
I am learning how to clarify my knowledge about the geography of New Jersey by reading a map legend and scale to answer text dependent questions about New Jersey.
objective.student_goal: I will use map features to answer questions.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how map features show information (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading) by using map vocabulary with bilingual gestures and a legend-guided organizer appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing short written answers that reference map symbols and scale.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
“There are lots of different kinds of maps, such as maps that show climate, weather, population, and historical information. In this last activity about maps, you will be provided with a “Fun Facts” about New Jersey Map.”

In order to answer the follow-up questions, you will need to read the map. Remember the map legend and scale will help you. Think, pair, share the answer to this question, "How do you read a map?"
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Pre-teach map vocabulary with Portuguese cognates: legenda (legend), escala (scale), símbolo (symbol). 2) Use quick Portuguese think time to identify one familiar symbol, then explain in English using a frame.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently. 
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis

co_teaching_model.rationale: Stations provide concrete opportunities to practice map-reading with a bilingual teacher supporting legend and scale use while others complete text-dependent questions.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: levels_3_4_dominant; some students need repeated S-L (speech-to-language) practice with map vocabulary and gestures.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce map symbols and gestures in Portuguese and English; model reading legend entries.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Pose the 'How do you read a map?' prompt and organize think-pair-share.
phase_plan.details: Use a large projected map and point to legend items as bilingual teacher labels them.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Guide small group through reading the Fun Facts map, modeling how to use scale to determine distances and how to interpret symbols.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead full-group example question walkthrough.
phase_plan.details: Stop for student paraphrase in both languages after each example.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a station where students answer map-dependent questions with bilingual prompts and a legend checklist; support Level 1-2 students with sentence starters.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate another station focused on applying scale and distance calculations with peer collaboration.
phase_plan.details: Use manipulatives (ruler/scale strip) at stations; rotate groups.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Have students verbally explain one map feature using the frame in English; provide quick corrective feedback in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect short written answers and remind students of homework.
phase_plan.details: Collect one sample response per group for formative data.

implementation_notes: Use existing 'Fun Facts' map as primary material; prepare legend checklists for stations.
Bilingual teacher prepares 6 symbol-gesture pairs and posts them near the station.
Limit new verbal input; focus on guided practice with existing map and question set.

ell_support.implementation: Teach 6 key terms with consistent gestures and Portuguese cognates; prompt students to use gesture when answering to reinforce meaning.
Provide a simple checklist (Symbol / Meaning / Where found) with Portuguese labels to guide students through question answers.
Allow students to think in Portuguese, share key idea with partner in Portuguese, then report one sentence in English using a frame.

special_needs_support: Provide enlarged map printouts or a tactile map for students with visual or processing needs.
Allow extra time and one-on-one check-ins for students who need repetition of scale calculations.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may struggle with applying their vocabulary knowledge to answer the questions (i.e. I know what a legend or a key is, but I do not know how to use it to get the answer).
Students may incorrectly answer the question because it provides them with “interesting but unimportant information” (See questions 6 and 8).

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese preposition use differs (e.g., 'em' can mean 'in/on/at'), which can cause confusion when students answer location questions in English (in the north, on the coast).
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach English location prepositions explicitly with map examples and contrastive practice (e.g., 'in the north' vs. 'on the coast'); provide mini-sentence frames using correct prepositions.

assessment.primary_assessment: Explanatory Writing: Using the SPO activity, explain to your reader why New Jersey is a fascinating place to visit.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short explanatory writing (SPO) referencing map features and at least one piece of evidence from the Fun Facts map.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain; ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing + Reading; Key Language Use: Explain (use map evidence to clarify a point). Target levels 2-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a map-scaffolded frame (Sentence starter + symbol to reference) and allow responses of 1-2 short sentences; permit initial planning in Portuguese.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide SPO organizer in English with Portuguese cue words; expect 2-3 sentences referencing map symbols/scale.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a short paragraph that uses map evidence and transition words; minimal L1 support.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess use of map evidence (explicit reference to a symbol or scale), clarity of explanation (logic and organization), and language control appropriate to level (phrase vs. sentence vs. paragraph).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing Fun Facts map and SPO organizer; no new materials required.

homework.original_content:
Reading Log
SIPPS Group 1: Extension Lesson 15
homework.family_connection: Peça a um familiar para olhar o mapa com você e pergunte onde está a praia ou a montanha mais próxima. Em inglês, escreva uma frase curta dizendo onde está e por que alguém deveria visitar. (Ask a family member to look at the map with you and ask where the nearest beach or mountain is. In English, write one short sentence saying where it is and why someone should visit.)

slots.subject: ELA/SS
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit 3 Lesson 5 
SIPPS: Extension Review
Lesson 11

objective.content_objective:
I can select the correct vocabulary word when given word meaning.
I can generate sentences that accurately include vocabulary terms.
objective.student_goal: I will choose words and write sentences correctly.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform by selecting vocabulary from definitions and producing sentences using target words (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Writing) by using picture-word matching, sentence frames, and bilingual previews appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing one or two clear sentences that show correct word use.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning  procedures.
Unit 3
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Display target vocabulary with Portuguese cognates (ex.: importante - important; informação - information). 2) Quick matching warm-up in Portuguese then repeat in English to link meaning.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Students will be provided assistance with fluent reading to help them practice on their own. 
Small Group Intruction
With ESL and Bilingual Teachers
Students can work in small groups to help assist them in fluent reading. 

Small group instruction.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching enables one station focused on vocabulary selection/matching and another on fluency practice; supports both decoding and expressive tasks.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Predominantly Levels 3-4 with some Level 2 students; stations allow targeted practice for vocabulary selection and sentence generation.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick vocabulary matching in Portuguese/English with gestures.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model a fluent read-aloud focusing on target words.
phase_plan.details: Use echo reading for one short paragraph.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a small group matching definitions to words and modeling sentence generation in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Conduct guided fluency practice with another small group.
phase_plan.details: Students practice reading target sentences aloud.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate word-selection station where students pick correct vocabulary for short definitions and compose sentences using frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Run fluency station focusing on phrasing and punctuation.
phase_plan.details: Rotate groups; bilingual teacher provides immediate corrective feedback.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support students to write one sentence using a target word with L1 prompts if needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect one-sentence samples and note use of vocabulary and punctuation.
phase_plan.details: Encourage self-correction strategies modeled earlier.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Invite a student to read a sentence aloud in Portuguese and then in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Highlight correct vocabulary use and a punctuation point.
phase_plan.details: Remind students of homework and spelling sentence task.

implementation_notes: Prepare word-definition cards and sentence frames; use index cards for quick matching.
Echo reading and choral reading used to build fluency.
Punctuation mini-lessons included at fluency station for transfer to reading aloud.
warnings: Students struggling with punctuation may need repeated short modeled practice; plan for extra brief mini-lessons.

ell_support.implementation: Provide picture prompts for each vocabulary word and a two-column card (Portuguese / English); students match and say the word in both languages before writing.
Teacher reads a sentence with target vocabulary; students echo and then read chorally to build fluency and pronunciation.
Provide frames: 'The ___ is ___.' and 'I see ___ in the story.' in Portuguese and English to scaffold sentence creation.
Teach students a simple cue (e.g., 'Check the picture') to self-correct when a word doesn't match the picture; model in both languages.

special_needs_support: Provide highlighter to mark target words in text and allow tracing letters for spelling support.
Permit student to point and record an oral sentence if writing is inaccessible.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may not be able to differentiate between the punctuation marks. 
Students may not be able to know when to use the punctuation marks. 
Students may not remember what to do when seeing a certain punctuation mark while reading.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese past-tense forms differ from English '-ed' endings; students may omit '-ed' or confuse tense (e.g., 'walk' vs 'walked').
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Provide explicit mini-lessons with choral practice of '-ed' endings, contrast with Portuguese past forms, and use gesture to mark past actions.

assessment.primary_assessment: Teacher will monitor the students ability to pick the right reading spot and read independently. Students will also be assessed on their ability to follow the routines and procedures and taught expectations.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Teacher running-record style observation of reading spot selection, fluency, and use of target vocabulary during independent reading.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Key Language Use: Inform; ELD domains: Reading/Speaking; Target Levels: 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to point to pictures and say single words; bilingual teacher prompts in Portuguese then elicits an English word.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide short frames for students to generate one or two sentences using target vocabulary; encourage underlining of target word in text.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect fluent reading with correct vocabulary use in sentences and self-correction for punctuation when prompted.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess correct selection of reading spot, accurate use of target vocabulary in an oral or written sentence, and evidence of following procedures; prioritize meaning and evidence of strategy use over perfect grammar.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No additional materials introduced; uses existing texts and observation routines.

homework.original_content:
Read for 20 minutes and log

Spelling (sentence writing)
homework.family_connection: Peça que a família escute seu filho ler uma sentença com a palavra do dia. In Portuguese, ask: 'O que essa palavra significa?' Then have the child say the sentence in English and write it in the log.

slots.subject: Science
slots.unit_lesson:
Properties of Matter
Lesson 4 Day 6

objective.content_objective: I am learning about the properties of different materials
objective.student_goal: I will describe and test a material property
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how material properties determine use (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Science) by conducting a simple investigation with bilingual supports and using sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing an observation record in their notebook that demonstrates cause-effect thinking.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to 2nd grade science curriculum starting with page 128.
 2nd Grade Science
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Show a quick demonstration (e.g., bend plastic vs. metal) and name the action in Portuguese then English (dobrar / bend). 2) Ask students to predict in Portuguese what will happen, then say the English prediction using a frame.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives

co_teaching_model.rationale: Stations allow the bilingual teacher to support investigation steps and language for students who need guided help while others work more independently; aligns with 2nd grade developmental needs.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Typical mix with Levels 3-4 dominant and some Levels 1-2; investigation requires scaffolded language and hands-on support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Review investigation vocabulary and demonstrate one step in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Remind students of safety and data recording expectations.
phase_plan.details: Students echo key verbs (test, bend, float) in both languages.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model how to record an observation in the notebook using bilingual sentence frames and a visual organizer.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate the testing procedure with one material sample.
phase_plan.details: Use gestures and pictures to show steps.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead the L1-support station: scaffold step-by-step testing, support vocabulary in Portuguese, and help students fill in one notebook entry.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Supervise two other stations where students perform tests and record findings with decreasing support.
phase_plan.details: Rotate every 8 minutes; final 1 minute to prepare notebooks for collection.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Collect one verbal report from a bilingual pair and restate in English on anchor chart.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect notebooks and give exit prompt.
phase_plan.details: Highlight one correct use of property vocabulary in English.

implementation_notes: Prepare simple test kits (cup of water for float test, small weights for bend test) and safety guidelines.
Bilingual teacher brings a pre-filled exemplar notebook entry showing Portuguese labels and an English sentence.
warnings: If limited materials, run fewer stations and have students rotate through observation only.

ell_support.implementation: Briefly highlight one language structure difference (e.g., noun-adjective order) when students write observations; provide side-by-side example in both languages.

ell_support.strategy_name: Mini Word Bank with Picture Support
ell_support.implementation: Provide a pocket-sized word bank (Portuguese + English + picture) for each station to support independent recording.
Bilingual teacher briefly conferences with students at station to help craft one English sentence to include in notebook.
Use gesture-supported choral repetition after each test to reinforce verbs and properties in both languages.

special_needs_support: Provide step-by-step pictorial checklist for the investigation and allow adult support for recording.
Allow alternative response: draw results instead of writing for students with expressive language delays.
materials: Simple test kits (water cup, small weights, safe bend samples)

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle with completing the investigation independently

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese allows subject pronoun omission more frequently than English; students may omit subjects in English sentences (e.g., 'Is hard' instead of 'It is hard').
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Provide sentence frames that include the subject (e.g., 'It is ___') and practice full-sentence responses in choral and paired activities.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will complete p.48 in their Be a Scientist Notebook.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Notebook page p.48 (investigation recording) - teacher collects student pages
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain + ELD-LA.2-3 (Writing/Speaking) - target Levels 1-4; product: investigation notes and one English explanatory sentence.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow drawing plus Portuguese label and one English word; bilingual teacher can scribe an English sentence during conference.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame and word bank; students write one English explanatory sentence with teacher support.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students write two-sentence explanation linking property to function (e.g., 'This material is hard, so it does not bend easily.').

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess accuracy of observed result, correct link between property and outcome, and presence of at least one English sentence or phrase appropriate to level.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses Be a Scientist Notebook p.48 and classroom investigation materials only.

homework.family_connection: Peça à criança para mostrar um objeto usado em casa e descrever em português uma propriedade (por exemplo: 'dura'). Em inglês, pratique juntos dizendo: 'It is hard.'

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit 3: Lesson 8
What is an inch?

objective.content_objective: I can measure in inches.
objective.student_goal: I will measure using inches accurately.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how to measure small lengths in inches (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using bilingual vocabulary supports, guided gestures, and ruler practice appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing short oral answers and labeled measurements.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 3: Measuring Length
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Step 1: Show a familiar small object and the Portuguese term 'polegada' while demonstrating one inch on a ruler. Step 2: Students repeat the English phrase 'one inch' and Portuguese 'uma polegada'. Portuguese cognates/terms: polegada, medir (note: not cognates but key L1 terms to map).

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Small group
instruction,
Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching provides hands-on practice with small-unit measurement while allowing bilingual teacher to scaffold precise vocabulary (inch/polegada) and ruler use.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Predominantly Levels 3-4 with some Level 1 students needing modeled L1 demonstrations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce the inch with a concrete demonstration in Portuguese and English using a small object.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model locating one-inch segments on a ruler and counting markings.
phase_plan.details: Use a large visual ruler and gesture to show the inch interval.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Teach a short bilingual chant/direction for measuring in inches and provide phrase cards.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate measuring several small classroom objects and modeling language.
phase_plan.details: Show how to align the ruler and where to read.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a guided station with rulers and manipulatives, prompting students in Portuguese then encouraging English production.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Oversee measurement accuracy station and check student recordings.
phase_plan.details: Rotate students through measuring tasks with peer checks.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask two students to report measurements using English frame supported by Portuguese if needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Confirm learning target and collect quick evidence.
phase_plan.details: Use show-me cards for a fast exit.

implementation_notes: Have rulers available at Station B and pre-mark one-inch intervals for students who need it.
Use peer-check prompts in Portuguese for Level 1 students before English reporting.

ell_support.implementation: Provide small cards showing '1 inch' with a picture and the Portuguese '1 polegada' to match with objects; students place card next to measured item.
Offer step-by-step bilingual frames for using a ruler (line up, read, record) and have students repeat with partner.
Use a consistent hand cue for 'inch' and point to markings while speaking the term in both languages.

special_needs_support: Provide pre-marked rulers with tactile bumps and allow oral responses if writing is difficult.

misconceptions.original_content: Misconceptions: Students may struggle with relating one cm cube to 10 cm cubes.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may omit subject pronouns (e.g., 'É 5 polegadas' vs. 'It is 5 inches'), leading to English fragments when answering measurement questions.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full English responses using the subject 'It is ___ inches' and practice choral repetition, then have students produce full sentences in partners.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.3.2 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool Down: quick hands-on check where students measure a small item and either show the number of inches using cubes or state the measurement aloud.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain + ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking; target Levels 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to show measurement with cubes or point to pre-marked inches; accept Portuguese or single-word English responses.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame 'It is ___ inches.' and ask students to record or say the sentence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Ask students to compare two measured items and explain which is longer using a connecting word.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Check correct reading of ruler marks (math) and use of the unit term 'inches' or 'inch' in an English phrase (language).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; use small rulers, counting cubes, numberlines, and visuals already referenced.

homework.original_content: Measure one small object at home using a ruler and tell someone its length.
homework.family_connection: Peça a uma pessoa em casa para ajudar a medir um objeto pequeno com uma régua e diga a medida em voz alta. / Ask someone at home to help measure a small object with a ruler and say the length out loud.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: LESSON 3: ADD YOUR WAY  3.3.3 Teacher Guide

objective.content_objective: SWBAT use strategies to add within 1,000.
objective.student_goal: I will add numbers within 1,000 using strategies.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain addition strategies and reasoning for sums within 1,000 (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing) by using number-talk frames, comparative language, and bilingual modeling appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a written equation plus a short explanation.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Number Talk: Hundreds, Tens, and Ones (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Say 'hundreds, tens, ones' in Portuguese (centenas, dezenas, unidades) and point to a base-ten diagram. 2) Ask students in Portuguese which place they'd add first, then repeat responses in English using a frame.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Activity 1: Strategies to Add (25 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to add within 1,000, using any strategy that makes sense to them.

Activity 2: Two Ways to Add (10 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to see that they can start adding from the greatest or the least place-value unit and still get the same sum.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Team Teaching allows both teachers to model multiple addition strategies in English and Portuguese, co-constructing reasoning for diverse proficiency levels.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class with a spread of Levels 2–4 and a few students needing extra conceptual scaffolds; co-teaching enables immediate bilingual contrast and extension.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a bilingual number talk focusing on place-value language in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Prompt students with questions that elicit strategy names and choices.
phase_plan.details: Use one example displayed with blocks and ask 'How did you add?'

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model adding by grouping tens in Portuguese and translate steps to English, highlighting connectors.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model adding by alternative strategy (adding ones first) in English and compare results.
phase_plan.details: Show both methods on chart paper side-by-side.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with small groups needing language support; provide sentence stems and allow short L1 planning.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Work with groups ready for strategy extension and error analysis.
phase_plan.details: Students practice two strategies and record which they prefer and why.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate a quick bilingual share: one student says method in Portuguese, another in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick checks for understanding.
phase_plan.details: Use thumbs-up/side signals for self-assessment.

implementation_notes: Prepare two anchor charts showing both addition methods with bilingual labels.
Use paired recording sheets so students can compare methods side-by-side.
Coordinate who models which strategy during Input to avoid repetition.

ell_support.implementation: Provide number-line templates students can use to show jumps; bilingual teacher models jumps and labels in Portuguese, then students label in English.
Have students solve the same problem two ways and use a short frame: 'Method A gives ___, Method B gives ___; both are the same because ___.' Offer Portuguese frame for planning.
Pair a student with stronger English with one who benefits from Portuguese explanations; allow short peer-teaching in L1 then English practice.
Display frames for explaining steps (I added ___ to ___ to get ___); encourage rehearsal in Portuguese then English writing.

special_needs_support: Provide step-by-step checklist for multi-step addition and allow manipulatives for each step.
Allow demonstration of solution with physical objects instead of written output when needed.
materials: Anchor charts for two addition strategies

misconceptions.original_content: Misconception: Students may not line up place value correctly when applying addition algorithms.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese past tense markers differ from English; when students explain steps they may drop or misuse English past tense ('I add' instead of 'I added'), which can make reasoning less clear.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Provide a simple timeline frame (First..., Then..., Finally...) with Portuguese parallel and practice oral rehearsal before writing.

assessment.primary_assessment: 3.3.3 Cool-down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down check: two short addition problems within 1,000—one solved by student-preferred strategy and one solved by alternate strategy (paper).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain; ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing; targeted levels 1-5

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Offer a partially completed number-line or organizer and allow student to indicate steps with drawings; permit one short English phrase or Portuguese planning.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames for explanation and expect a sentence or short paragraph describing the chosen strategy in English.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a clear explanation comparing two strategies using connectors and supporting evidence.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for correct computation and then apply a 0–2 language score for use of frames, clarity of reasoning, and correct math vocabulary.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses only manipulatives and recording sheets available in class.

homework.original_content: 3.3 Section A: Practice Problems: Problem 9
homework.family_connection: Peça ao seu filho(a) para mostrar duas maneiras diferentes de somar o mesmo número usando objetos domésticos. Em seguida, peça que explique em português qual método ele(a) preferiu e por quê.

slots.subject: ELA
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 2- Maps and Globes Lesson 14 Day 2

objective.content_objective:
I am learning how to use map features to understand information.
I am learning how to clarify my knowledge about the geography of New Jersey by reading a map legend and scale to answer text dependent questions about New Jersey.
objective.student_goal: I will use map features to answer questions.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain map information and answer text-dependent questions (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading) by using bilingual previews, legend-based frames, and guided academic discourse appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing accurate answers that cite map symbols or scale.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
“There are lots of different kinds of maps, such as maps that show climate, weather, population, and historical information. In this last activity about maps, you will be provided with a “Fun Facts” about New Jersey Map.”

In order to answer the follow-up questions, you will need to read the map. Remember the map legend and scale will help you. Think, pair, share the answer to this question, "How do you read a map?"
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Revisit Portuguese cognates: legenda (legend), escala (scale), símbolo (symbol). 2) Quick bilingual warmup: students label three symbols in Portuguese, then translate labels to English with teacher support.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently. 
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports varied WIDA levels with a bilingual teacher facilitating legend/scale application at one station while other stations complete the Map and 10 Questions task.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: levels_3_4_dominant with a mix of emerging and expanding multilinguals; station rotations allow targeted scaffolding.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Review legend terms and model one map question in Portuguese and English; ensure students know the location of scale on the map.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Display the Map and 10 Questions and clarify task expectations.
phase_plan.details: Emphasize where to locate the Answer Key (last page) for teacher reference only.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate using the scale to answer a sample question and show how to cite the symbol from the legend.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model think-aloud for question types that require distinguishing important vs. interesting details.
phase_plan.details: Use one sample question to model steps for all students.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate a supported station where students answer 3-4 map questions with bilingual prompts and frames; circulate to confer with groups needing extra language support.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Oversee independent completion of Map and 10 Questions by other groups and provide feedback.
phase_plan.details: Rotate groups through supported station; ensure Level 1-2 students have access to shortened prompts.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead brief share-out of one group's answer and highlight correct use of legend/scale language in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect Map and 10 Questions responses for formative review.
phase_plan.details: Remind students of homework and SIPPS group rotations.

implementation_notes: Primary assessment ('Map and 10 Questions') is used as station task; bilingual teacher preps legend-checklists and sentence frames.
Do not provide students with the Answer Key; teacher uses it for quick checks after collection.
Use existing maps and the Map and 10 Questions sheet only; no new handouts.
warnings: If planning time is limited, reduce rotation complexity and keep bilingual teacher at the same station to maintain support fidelity.

ell_support.implementation: Give students a short checklist to tick off (Symbol / What it means / Where found) in Portuguese + English for each Map and 10 Questions item they answer.
Provide starters such as 'The map shows __ because __.' and 'The symbol for __ means __.' in English with Portuguese translation; students use starters to form answers.
Use structured partner talk where one student explains in Portuguese then the partner reformulates in English using frames; rotate roles to build output.

special_needs_support: Provide one-on-one conferencing and allow oral responses for students with written language needs.
Offer simplified question stems or reduced number of items for students requiring modified workload.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may struggle with applying their vocabulary knowledge to answer the questions (i.e. I know what a legend or a key is, but I do not know how to use it to get the answer).
Students may incorrectly answer the question because it provides them with “interesting but unimportant information” (See questions 6 and 8).

linguistic_note.note: Differences in Portuguese preposition use (e.g., 'em') can cause students to select incorrect English prepositions for location-based answers.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Provide explicit mini-lessons contrasting English location prepositions with map examples and require practice sentences using correct prepositions before answering.

assessment.primary_assessment: Assessment: Assessment: Map and 10 Questions (Answer Key is on last page)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Map and 10 Questions (written short answers referencing map features).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain; ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading + Writing; Key Language Use: Explain/Inform; target levels 2-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Use a bilingual checklist and allow oral response recorded by a partner or teacher; expect 1-2 word/phrase answers with symbol reference.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence starters and legend checklist; expect short sentences citing symbol or scale.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect complete sentences with map evidence and brief explanation; minimal L1 support.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Evaluate correct use of legend/scale evidence, relevance of answer (important vs. interesting details), and language clarity appropriate to level band.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No additional materials introduced; overlay uses the existing Map and 10 Questions sheet and class map only.

homework.original_content:
Reading Log
SIPPS Group 2: Extension Lesson 15
homework.family_connection: Comente com sua família um fato que você aprendeu no mapa (em português) e depois escreva a versão em inglês no seu Reading Log. (Tell your family one fact you learned from the map in Portuguese, then write the English version in your Reading Log.)

slots.subject: ELA/SS
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit 3 Lesson 6
SIPPS: Extension Review 12

objective.content_objective:
I can preview a text, paying attention to the title, cover, illustrations, and opening.
I can cite evidence from the text to support an interpretation.
objective.student_goal: I will preview text and cite supporting evidence.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain an interpretation by previewing text features and citing evidence (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading) by using preview–review, picture analysis, and sentence frames with bilingual supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a short explanation referencing text features.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide
Unit 3
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Point out cognates on the cover: ilustração - illustration; título - title. 2) In Portuguese, ask families: 'Como a capa nos ajuda a entender?' then have students answer in English using a frame.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Stories read aloud to a small group. 

Small group instruction 
With ESL and Bilingual Teachers

Supporting resources provided

Sentence stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports explicit preview of text features in one station while other stations practice citing evidence and independent reading.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class contains a majority of developing ELLs (Levels 3-4) with a few emerging readers (Levels 1-2); stations allow differentiation.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a cover-walk station in Portuguese/English highlighting title, cover, and illustrations.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce how to preview text and set a reading focus for evidence-finding.
phase_plan.details: Use a quick chart 'Title - Picture - First Sentence' to scaffold previewing.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model thinking aloud in Portuguese to identify clues from illustrations, then translate thought process to English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read the opening pages and point out evidence that supports an interpretation.
phase_plan.details: Students mark evidence with a sticky dot.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a station where students work with picture cards to match evidence to an interpretation using bilingual sentence stems.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Guide another station focused on writing one evidence-based sentence in English.
phase_plan.details: Rotate after 7 minutes each; use exemplar anchors.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Confer with students needing language support to help them write one evidence sentence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect student responses and note evidence use.
phase_plan.details: Students complete a short evidence strip to glue into their reading log.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Invite a student to show their evidence strip and say the sentence in Portuguese and English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Reinforce the steps of previewing and citing evidence.
phase_plan.details: Remind students of spelling practice for homework.

implementation_notes: Prepare evidence strips and sticky dots before lesson.
Use an anchor chart with preview steps in both languages.
Coordinate roles: bilingual teacher leads preview & translation; primary teacher collects comprehension evidence.

ell_support.implementation: Guide students through title, cover, and illustrations with prompts in Portuguese then English; use a two-column chart to note clues and predicted meaning.
Provide small sentence strips where students write one piece of evidence and one-sentence interpretation using frames; bilingual teacher models with examples.
Model how illustrations give clues (think-aloud) first in Portuguese, then convert to English statements students practice repeating.
Students work in pairs to translate a short evidence sentence from Portuguese to English, discussing word choices and cognates.

special_needs_support: Provide pre-highlighted images and allow students to point to evidence instead of writing.
Allow use of drawing to show predicted interpretation if writing is a barrier.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may not be able to read independently. 

Some students may not be able to demonstrate the characteristics of a fluent reader.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese preposition use differs (e.g., 'dependem de' vs 'depend on'); students may misuse prepositions when explaining evidence relationships in English.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Explicitly teach common English prepositions used in explanations (because, since, due to) with bilingual examples and practice combining cause/evidence phrases.

assessment.primary_assessment: The teacher will observe the students ability to use their decoding skills while working independently.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Observation checklist during independent decoding and evidence-finding tasks (anecdotal notes and one collected evidence strip).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Key Language Use: Explain; ELD domains: Reading/Writing; Target Levels: 1-4.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to point to evidence and say a single word or short phrase in English after discussing in Portuguese.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide bilingual sentence frames and a word bank; students write one sentence citing evidence.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a two-sentence response linking preview clues to an interpretation with appropriate connectors.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Look for explicit reference to a text feature (title/illustration/opening) and at least one piece of evidence; at lower levels accept phrase-level responses.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses only existing materials (reading texts, sticky dots, sentence stems); no new tasks introduced.

homework.original_content:
Read for 20 minutes and log

Spelling (5 times each)
homework.family_connection: Peça que seu filho mostre a capa do livro em português e diga uma pista que encontrou. Then have the child tell one sentence in English about what the cover suggests and record it in the reading log.

slots.subject: Science
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit A
Chapter 1
Lesson 8

objective.content_objective: Students will learn the steps for resolving conflict
objective.student_goal: I will use steps to resolve a problem respectfully
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Argue and Explain conflict resolution steps (ELD-LA.2-3.Argue.Speaking) by practicing sentence stems, role-play in Portuguese→English pairs, and using bilingual sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a short role-play or poster that demonstrates sequence and respectful language.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Unit A
Chapter 1
Lesson 7
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Introduce key conflict vocabulary with cognates and translations (problema / problem; acordo / agree; ouvir / listen). 2) Use a short culturally familiar scenario (playground dispute) and ask students in Portuguese how to solve it, then map responses to English steps.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives
Sentence Stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports language rehearsal and role-play in small groups, allowing bilingual teacher to scaffold L1 phrasing and translate key sentence stems into English.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class likely includes a range of WIDA levels; stations allow targeted practice of social language and sequences for conflict resolution.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce 4-step conflict routine in Portuguese and English with gestures and pictorial sequence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model a brief role-play demonstrating one step.
phase_plan.details: Students repeat steps chorally.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Teach sentence stems in Portuguese then scaffold to English (e.g., 'I feel ___ when ___; Can we ___?').
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model using stems in a teacher-led role-play.
phase_plan.details: Display stems on chart in both languages.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run station for practice: scaffold L1 rehearsals, support students to produce final English line, and help create a small poster listing steps in both languages.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Supervise other stations where students do guided role-plays and peer feedback.
phase_plan.details: Rotate pairs every 8 minutes; final minutes used to display posters.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask volunteers to perform a short two-line role-play using one stem; restate in English and praise use of polite language.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect posters and note students who need additional practice.
phase_plan.details: Post one exemplar poster for classroom reference.

implementation_notes: Create simple pictorial four-step chart (listen, say feeling, suggest solution, agree).
Bilingual teacher pre-writes sentence stems in Portuguese + English on cue cards for Level 1-2 students.
warnings: If class contains many newcomers, allow extra rehearsal time in Portuguese before expecting English production.

ell_support.implementation: Provide stems on index cards in Portuguese + English (e.g., 'I feel __ when __.' / 'I feel __ quando __.') for role-play practice; rehearse aloud.
Students practice scenario first in Portuguese then in English, with bilingual teacher modeling translation and polite expressions.
Use a 4-step pictorial chart with short bilingual captions; students point and practice steps during stations.
Teach simple feedback frame: 'You said __. I liked __.' in Portuguese + English; pairs practice giving one feedback comment.

special_needs_support: Allow students to use picture cues or a poster to follow role-play steps and reduce memory load.
Provide sentence stem strips glued into notebooks for students needing language prompts.
materials: Resolve Conflicts worksheet (as referenced by primary teacher)
Index cards with bilingual sentence stems
Be a Scientist Notebook (if referenced for behavior journaling)

misconceptions.original_content: Vocabulary

linguistic_note.note: Some English words related to social interactions may look similar to Portuguese but mean different things (false cognates); students may assume identical meaning.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach target social vocabulary with clear examples and role-plays showing correct usage and contrast with Portuguese near-cognates.

assessment.primary_assessment: “Resolve Conflicts” worksheet

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Worksheet activity (written responses and drawing) - same worksheet collected by teacher
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Argue/Explain + ELD-LA.2-3 (Speaking/Writing) - target Levels 1-4; product: short written/drawn responses and performed role-play

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow drawing and one-word or Portuguese responses with teacher scribing one English sentence during conference; provide stem cards.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stems and pictorial prompts; students write or copy one short sentence in English.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students write two simple sentences describing steps and perform a short role-play using stems with minimal prompts.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Evaluate comprehension of sequence (can list steps), appropriate use of polite language, and attempt to produce at least one English sentence or phrase per level expectation.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses the existing worksheet and classroom visuals only.

homework.original_content: NA
homework.family_connection: Peça à criança para praticar uma pequena cena com um membro da família (2 linhas) em português, depois tentar dizer uma linha em inglês usando o molde: 'I feel __ when __.'

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: Lesson: 9 From feet to inches

objective.content_objective: I can measure from inches to feet.
objective.student_goal: I will measure using inches and feet.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain measurement conversion between inches and feet (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using bilingual conversion frames, manipulatives, and numberline visuals appropriate for WIDA levels 1-5, producing labeled comparisons and conversion statements.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 3: Measuring Length
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Step 1: Show a one-foot object and say 'pé' and 'foot' aloud; Step 2: Demonstrate stacking 12 one-inch cubes to make one foot, labeling in Portuguese and English. Portuguese cognates/terms: pé (foot), polegada (inch).

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Small group
instruction,
Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching allows targeted conversion practice and small-group bilingual scaffolding for students learning to connect inches to feet.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class with many students at Levels 3-4 and some Levels 1-2 who need hands-on conversion modeling.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Review 'inch' and 'foot' with concrete stacking demo in Portuguese and English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model conversion: '12 inches = 1 foot' using cubes and a visual chart.
phase_plan.details: Use a visual conversion anchor chart displayed.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Teach a bilingual conversion chant and conversion frame '___ inches = ___ feet'.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate converting two examples and show steps on numberline.
phase_plan.details: Use guided questioning and gestures to show grouping.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead Station A where students group cubes into feet while narrating in Portuguese then in English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead Station B where students record conversions and solve 1-2 quick conversion problems.
phase_plan.details: Rotate through a visual matching station for additional practice.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Prompt a student to explain one conversion using the English frame supported by Portuguese cueing.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Confirm accuracy and restate learning target.
phase_plan.details: Use a quick thumbs-up check for comprehension.

implementation_notes: Create a conversion anchor chart (12 inches = 1 foot) and display at each station.
Use grouping mats for counting sets of 12 cubes to form feet to reduce counting load.

ell_support.implementation: Use a bilingual anchor chart showing groups of 12 inches = 1 foot with picture cues; practice chanting conversions in pairs.
Provide mats for students to make groups of 12 cubes and label groups in Portuguese then English to build conceptual understanding.
Give frames: '___ inches is equal to ___ feet.' Model and have students complete frames aloud or in writing.

special_needs_support: Provide pre-grouped sets of 12 cubes for students who struggle with counting.
Allow oral demonstration instead of written recording for students with fine-motor difficulties.

misconceptions.original_content:
Misconceptions: Students will need support creating ruler. 

Students may struggle spacing out ruler.

linguistic_note.note: Students may confuse prepositional phrases when describing measurements (e.g., 'from the end' vs. Portuguese constructions), affecting clarity in conversion explanations.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach and practice bilingual frames that include prepositions and anchor phrases: 'from the end to the mark / do início até a marca'.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.3.3 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool Down: short conversion problems (oral or written) where students convert inches to feet or vice versa and explain their method briefly.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain + ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking/Writing; target Levels 1-5.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Use manipulatives to show conversion (point to groups of 12) and allow Portuguese explanation or one-word English response with teacher scribing.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and conversion chart; expect a short sentence or labeled drawing.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a brief written explanation using conversion language and connectors.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess conversion accuracy (math) and use of conversion vocabulary and frames (language). Provide partial credit for correct process even if numeric answer is approximate.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; use grouping mats, counting cubes, numberlines, and conversion chart already referenced.

homework.original_content: Practice measuring a longer object at home in inches and then group into feet.
homework.family_connection: Peça para medir um objeto mais comprido em casa (por exemplo, uma almofada) em polegadas e depois mostrar quantos pés isso dá. / Measure a longer item at home in inches and then show how many feet that equals.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: LESSON 4: INTRODUCTION TO ADDITION ALGORITHMS  3.3.4 Teacher Guide

objective.content_objective: SWBAT interpret addition algorithms and use an algorithm to calculate sums within 1,000.
objective.student_goal: I will use an algorithm to add within 1,000.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain and apply an addition algorithm for sums within 1,000 (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing) by using algorithm steps, sentence frames, and L1 previews appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a written algorithmic solution and brief justification.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Which Three Go Together: 247 (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Introduce 'algorithm' in Portuguese (algoritmo) and relate to step-by-step recipes familiar in students' lives. 2) Show a quick Portuguese explanation of the steps (somar unidades, tens, centenas) then repeat in English.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Activity 1: What is an Algorithm? (20 minutes)
In this activity, students use their knowledge of base-ten representations and place value to make sense of two addition algorithms.

Activity 2: Try an Algorithm (20 minutes)
The purpose of this activity is for students to try the algorithms they saw earlier in the lesson. The important thing is that they combine hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, and ones and ones, which should be a familiar idea from grade 2.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Stations support focused practice on algorithm steps with targeted bilingual scaffolds for students who need concrete modeling while allowing extension tasks for others.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Majority Levels 3–4 with some students needing repetition of algorithm vocabulary in Portuguese; stations allow multiple re-exposures.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce the term 'algorithm/algoritmo' and preview step language in Portuguese.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Pose the guiding question and model the first algorithm step in English.
phase_plan.details: Use an example number (247) to demonstrate step sequencing.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model algorithm steps slowly in Portuguese with gestures and connect each step to place-value blocks.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model the same algorithm in English and show notation for carrying/ regrouping.
phase_plan.details: Show both algorithms side-by-side on chart paper.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support a station focusing on step-by-step guided practice with manipulatives and bilingual sentence stems.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate a station with algorithm challenge problems and error-analysis tasks.
phase_plan.details: Three stations: guided algorithm practice, independent algorithm try, and error-analysis extension.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a quick two-sentence bilingual summary of the algorithm steps with student repetition.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick checks and note students needing reteach.
phase_plan.details: Students write one algorithm step in English using the frame.

implementation_notes: Prepare step-by-step algorithm visuals with bilingual labels; have manipulatives at guided station.
Use a visible timer for station rotations and ensure at least one rotation includes bilingual teacher support.
Coordinate who records student think-alouds for error-analysis station.

ell_support.implementation: Map each algorithm step with a short Portuguese explanation and an English frame; students first practice describing the step in Portuguese, then in English using frames.
Highlight differences in step-order phrasing between Portuguese and English (e.g., 'add tens' vs 'adicionar as dezenas') and provide side-by-side phrases for practice.
Provide common algorithm error examples and a bilingual checklist students use to find and correct mistakes at the error-analysis station.
Give frames such as 'First I ___, then I ___, finally I ___' with Portuguese parallels; require students to write the English frame when recording algorithm steps.

special_needs_support: Provide a step-by-step algorithm strip (with spaces to show regrouping) that students can move manipulatives along.
Allow students who struggle with writing to record a short oral explanation using the sentence frame with teacher transcribing.
materials: Algorithm step charts with bilingual labels

misconceptions.original_content: Misconception: Students may not line up place value correctly when applying addition algorithms.

linguistic_note.note: The English word 'actual' can be confused with Portuguese 'atual' (which means 'current'), leading students to misread word-problem language or algorithm step explanations if 'actual' appears in texts.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Avoid using 'actual' in prompts; when it appears, explicitly clarify meaning and provide a Portuguese equivalent; teach students to ask for clarification of unfamiliar words.

assessment.primary_assessment: 3.3.4 Cool-down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down: one algorithm problem within 1,000 requiring use of standard addition algorithm and a one-sentence explanation (paper).
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain; ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Writing; targeted levels 1-5

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide an algorithm strip with guided prompts and allow student to show steps with manipulatives; accept number-only answers plus a one-word English label.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame for explanation and expect a short English sentence describing one step; allow Portuguese planning notes.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect full written algorithm with clear English justification and correct use of algorithm vocabulary.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Primary score based on correct algorithmic sum; language lens awards points for use of frames, precise vocabulary, and clarity of step explanation (0–2 points).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials or task changes; overlay uses manipulatives and the standard cool-down format only.

homework.original_content: 3.3 Section A: Practice Problems: Problem 10
homework.family_connection: Peça para seu filho(a) mostrar como fazer uma adição com agrupamento usando objetos (por exemplo, contas). Pergunte em português quais passos ele(a) fez e peça que desenhe os passos em inglês.

slots.subject: ELA
slots.unit_lesson: No School

slots.subject: ELA/SS
slots.unit_lesson:
Unit 3 Lesson 7
SIPPS: Extension Review 13

objective.content_objective:
I I can retell a story by completing a story map. 
I can support an explanation using the definition of a key term and text evidence.
objective.student_goal: I will retell and support ideas with evidence.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Narrate and Explain by retelling a story with a story map and supporting explanations with a key term and text evidence (ELD-LA.2-3.Narrate/Explain.Speaking) by using story maps, cognate awareness, and sentence frames with bilingual supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-5, producing an oral or written retell that includes one evidence sentence.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide
Unit 3
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Present cognates: mapa - map; recontar - retell; evidência - evidence. 2) Model a short retell in Portuguese, then have students practice the same retell in English using the story map boxes.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Stories read aloud to a small group. 

Small group instruction 
With ESL and Bilingual Teachers

Supporting resources provided

Sentence stems

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports retell practice with a dedicated story-map station and an evidence-citation station so students can both narrate and explain.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed proficiency with Levels 2-4 majority and some students approaching Level 5; stations allow differentiation and targeted L1 support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model filling the story map boxes (beginning/middle/end) in Portuguese, then translate to English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the story map and the day's retell goal.
phase_plan.details: Use an exemplar retell to show expectations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with a small group to retell orally using Portuguese cues and English frames; support vocabulary.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read the story aloud emphasizing sequence and key events.
phase_plan.details: Students listen for events to place on map.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a station where students complete the story map with bilingual labels and create one evidence sentence using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead fluency/retell practice at another station; students practice oral retell to a partner.
phase_plan.details: Rotate stations every 7 minutes; use visual timers.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Confer with students who need help shaping their evidence sentence; prompt in Portuguese then support English production.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect story maps and note retell completeness and evidence presence.
phase_plan.details: Students complete final sentence on their map.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Invite one student to retell a short sequence in Portuguese and then in English; highlight use of evidence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize rubric expectations for retell and evidence.
phase_plan.details: Remind students of weekend reading log.

implementation_notes: Prepare story-map templates and sentence-stem cards in both languages.
Use quick peer feedback protocol: Praise–Fix–Share for retell practice.
Collect one exemplar per group for formative assessment.

ell_support.implementation: Provide a 3-box story map with labels in Portuguese and English; students fill boxes with drawings and a short sentence using a frame.
Give frames: 'First ___. Then ___. Finally ___.' and 'I know this because ___.' in both languages to scaffold sequence and evidence.
Highlight cognates on the story map (mapa/map, evidência/evidence) to speed word recognition and support transfer.
Students retell to a partner first in Portuguese then in English; partner gives one specific piece of evidence they heard.

special_needs_support: Allow student to draw sequence with teacher scribing the retell sentence.
Provide sentence starters and allow oral retell recorded by the teacher.

misconceptions.original_content:
Some students may not know which strategy to use in order to self correct. 

Some students may just read words that make sense but are not actually on the page.

linguistic_note.note: Some Portuguese words look similar to English but mean different things (false cognates); students might misread words assuming identical meaning.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach a short false-cognate list (e.g., 'actual' false friends) and use guided comparison activities to clarify meanings before independent reading.

assessment.primary_assessment: Teacher will assess students reading while paying close attention to the punctuation marks.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Formative check of story map completion and one evidence sentence; teacher listens to oral retell and notes punctuation awareness.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Key Language Use: Narrate + Explain; ELD domains: Speaking/Writing; Target Levels: 1-5.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow oral retell in Portuguese then elicit one English phrase with teacher transcription; focus on sequence rather than punctuation.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and highlight punctuation cues; students write one evidence sentence using a frame.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Students produce a written retell with correct sentence boundaries and at least one evidence sentence using connectors like 'because'.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess retell completeness (beginning/middle/end), presence of at least one evidence sentence, and growing awareness of punctuation; award language-appropriate credit.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new tasks or materials introduced; uses existing story maps, sentence stems, and reading logs.

homework.original_content:
Read for 20 minutes and log

Spelling (ABC Order)

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: Lesson 10: Measure with a torn tape

objective.content_objective: I can measure without lining up to 0.
objective.student_goal: I will estimate and measure offset lengths.
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain and estimate measurements when tools are offset (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking) by using bilingual estimation strategies, ruler-offset frames, and manipulatives appropriate for WIDA levels 1-5, producing labeled measurements and short explanations.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 3: Measuring Length
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: Step 1: Show a torn tape or ruler with missing 0 and say 'fita rasgada' and 'torn tape' aloud. Step 2: Model how to measure by marking a start point and counting from that mark, saying steps in Portuguese then English. Portuguese cognates/terms: fita, medir, estimar.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Small group
instruction,
Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching permits targeted guided practice on offset measurement strategies while bilingual teacher previews methods in Portuguese for students who need it.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with some Levels 1-2 requiring explicit bilingual demonstration of offset techniques.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate offset measurement in Portuguese and English with a torn tape example.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model step-by-step how to mark a start point and count from any point.
phase_plan.details: Use a visible example that shows not starting at 0.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Teach bilingual frame 'Start at ___, count ___ more' and practice as a choral response.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Show two examples and how to record an accurate measurement despite offset.
phase_plan.details: Use guided questioning to check student reasoning.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a station practicing offset measurement using torn tape and manipulatives with L1 prompts.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Lead a station focusing on recording and estimating measurements with numberlines.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate and compare estimation strategies.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask a student to explain an offset measurement using both languages with the English frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick evidence and reaffirm the estimation strategy.
phase_plan.details: Use show-me or thumbs signals to confirm understanding.

implementation_notes: Prepare a torn tape example at one station and intact rulers at another; ensure materials are identical to those referenced in lessons.
Use bilingual verbal cues at the start of each rotation for Level 1-2 students.

ell_support.implementation: Provide a frame 'Start at ___, then count ___ marks' in both languages; model with a torn tape and have students practice in pairs.
Teach a two-step estimation routine (guess, check) with prompts in Portuguese then English; students verbalize their guess and process.
Provide sticky notes to mark start points on torn tape; students count from the mark and record the number using bilingual labels.

special_needs_support: Allow students extra time and provide a recording template to reduce working memory load.
Use larger tactile markers and allow oral demonstration for students who struggle with fine motor tasks.
materials: Torn tape / example offset measurement strips
Sticky notes for marking start points

misconceptions.original_content: Misconceptions: Students may struggle with how to estimate.

linguistic_note.note: Students may use Portuguese-English false friends or literal translations that change meaning when estimating or explaining (e.g., using 'actual' thinking it means 'currently' vs. 'real').
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach specific estimation vocabulary in both languages and provide example sentences showing correct usage (e.g., 'I estimate it is 8 inches' / 'Eu estimo que seja 8 polegadas').

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.3.4 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool Down: students estimate and then measure an offset item; they record estimate and measured result verbally or in writing.
bilingual_overlay.wida_mapping: Explain + ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Speaking/Writing; target Levels 1-5.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow students to indicate estimate with a gesture or point to a number on a pre-filled scale; accept Portuguese or English single-word responses.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide frame 'I estimate ___ inches; I measured ___ inches.' and expect a short written or oral response.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a sentence that explains the estimation strategy and the measurement result using connectors.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score estimation process and measurement accuracy separately; language scoring focuses on correct use of estimation vocabulary and unit terms.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; use torn tape examples, counting cubes, numberlines, and sticky notes already referenced.

homework.original_content: Try an estimation task at home: estimate an object's length then measure to check.
homework.family_connection: Peça a uma pessoa em casa para ajudar a fazer uma estimativa de comprimento de um objeto e depois medi-lo juntos. Escreva a estimativa e o resultado. / Ask someone at home to help estimate the length of an object and then measure it together. Write the estimate and the result.
